source_change_under_test.change: Default standard-EP hot split weight order to owner_compact so owner-compact helper rows no longer realign selected-order hot weights with W1/W2/W3 index_select.

materialized_gate.read: Corrected H64/N64/D128 rerun returned to the current torch-materializer control band, so the earlier 5.4k tok/GPU/s run was a sparse-MLA block-shape mismatch, not a MORI/native-layout regression. Still below retained 20-step AdamW baseline 8518.84 tok/GPU/s.

profile_ab.purpose: Compare torch owner-compact materialization with MORI compact materialization at the hot-helper ABI, with owner-compact weight order fixed to remove selected-order weight realignment.
profile_ab.caveat: These two timing runs used default seeding and therefore had different hot expert choices and remote row counts. A fixed-seed rerun was attempted but blocked because other jobs occupied GPUs 0-3 on both MI350 nodes. The rank-max stage ledger is still useful for mechanism sizing, not a promoted throughput comparison.

mori_buys: Owner-compact weight order removes selected-order hot-weight realignment; align_selected_hot_weight stage records are zero in both owner-compact runs.
MORI compact pack itself is small at this shape: about 1.49 ms init plus 5.30 ms pack rank-max in the step-2 ledger.
The whole dispatch hot_metadata parent is roughly equal between torch and MORI compact, about 18.0 ms vs 17.5 ms rank-max, so the pack edge is not the multi-hundred-ms bottleneck.
remaining_pay_side: These are shared remaining walls, not MORI-added overhead. The unchanged helper VJP dominates the hot-helper branch: helper_mlp_vjp_to_sorted_x about 283 ms, plus W3/W2/W1/activation autograd VJP about 166/88/82/98 ms rank-max in both torch-ownercompact and MORI-compact timing runs.

The return/scatter path is also shared: weighted_scatter_tokens is about 81 ms in the torch-ownercompact run and 91 ms in the MORI-compact run, while compact dx index_add is about 34 ms in both. The small difference is not a new MORI mechanism; the two runs had different hot expert choices and remote row counts.

The normal EP wrapper remains common to both paths: count_all_to_all about 98-107 ms, permute about 69-81 ms, combine wait about 136-156 ms, and weighted_scatter_add about 117-134 ms. MORI compact materialization was only swapping the hot-row materializer edge, not replacing normal EP dispatch/combine.
Hot-weight forward/reduce transport is not enough to promote by itself; W2 forward all-reduce and W3 backward grad all-reduce remain visible.
read.decision: Keep owner-compact weight order as the native ABI direction, but do not promote MORI compact materialization as a throughput win by itself. The measured MORI-specific added edge is only the compact-pack adapter, about 1.49 ms init plus 5.30 ms pack rank-max. The selected-order W1/W2/W3 realignment is removed by the owner-compact weight-order change, but the large shared helper VJP, scatter/return, and normal EP wrapper buckets remain. The next code target is native hot/cold/helper dispatcher layout plus compact helper VJP/return, not another pack-kernel tweak.

blocked_followup.details: Both do-sonle5-mi350-gpu and do-vunguyen-mi350-gpu had GPUs 0-3 occupied by other jobs when the seeded rerun was attempted.
